node.statement: N1.2 (INDUCTION, pure algebra). For every integer n >= 0, S_F^n j = j S_Phi^n (powers in A resp. B, composed with j: B->A); consequently for every n >= 0, (S_F^2 - I_A)^n j = j (S_Phi^2 - I_B)^n. DERIVATION (induction on n). Base n=0: S_F^0 j = I_A j = j = j I_B = j S_Phi^0 (powers are the unit; I_A j = j = j I_B as in N1.1). Inductive step: assume S_F^n j = j S_Phi^n. Then S_F^{n+1} j = S_F (S_F^n j) = S_F (j S_Phi^n) [inductive hypothesis] = (S_F j) S_Phi^n [associativity of composition, GT-hyp] = (j S_Phi) S_Phi^n [node 1.1] = j S_Phi^{n+1} [associativity]. By induction S_F^n j = j S_Phi^n for all n >= 0. For the (S^2 - I)^n form: S_F^2 j = j S_Phi^2 is the n=2 case, and since I_A j = j I_B, the element B_F := S_F^2 - I_A satisfies B_F j = (S_F^2 - I_A) j = j S_Phi^2 - j = j (S_Phi^2 - I_B) = j B_Phi with B_Phi := S_Phi^2 - I_B; then B_F^n j = j B_Phi^n by the SAME induction (replace S_F,S_Phi by B_F,B_Phi: base and step use only B_F j = j B_Phi and associativity). Hence (S_F^2 - I_A)^n j = j (S_Phi^2 - I_B)^n for all n>=0. Uses node 1.1, GT-hyp.
